rulings.Case Title: MATT NAWROCKI VS. PAUL BISAILLON DBA ESTHETE MANAGEMENT
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 10, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANT PAUL BISAILLON DBA ESTHETE MANAGEMENT's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Regarding the first, third and fourth causes of action, Plaintiff has raised triable issues regarding the amount that was to be contributed by Plaintiff and Defendant to the partnership and whether the $312,956 loan or loans were contributed by Plaintiff pursuant to section 2.5(iii) of the Agreement. (Nawrocki Decl. 2, 5-6.) Regarding the second cause of action, Plaintiff has raised a triable issue as to whether the loan giving rise to the Deed of Trust was a personal loan to Defendant that Defendant was obligated to repay. (Nawrocki Decl. 3.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: EMILY LLOYD VS. LAURA F SANDERSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 10, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANT LAURA SANDERSON's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Hearing required. Granted. Defendant met her burden of production on summary judgment, showing that the Complaint was filed one day after the statute of limitations expired. Plaintiff's opposition is procedurally defective for failing to include a separate statement of material facts. Cal.Rule of Court 3.1350(f). However, even disregarding this procedural defect, Plaintiff has not met her burden of production. Plaintiff's declaration states that she was denied information that would allow her to properly serve Defendant, not prevent her from timely filing a Complaint. Civil Procedure Code section 351 is inapplicable (see Vehicle Code 17463 and Hirsch v. Blish (1976) 76 Cal. App. 3d 163. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: KURT A. TINDLE VS. GRANT YEE LEONG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Attendance At Independent Medical Examination
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 10, 2013, Line 6, Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Attendance At Independent Medical Examination Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. Court would appreciate further clarification on scope of CCP §2032.220 in multi-defendant cases as to physical examinations "as of right." (See, e.g., Weil and Brown, et al., Cal Prac. Guide: Cal Proc Before Trial (Rutter Group 2012) §8:1522). Parties are also to meet and confer on scope of possible order permitting physical examination only but not permitting examination or questions pertaining to plaintiff's mental, psychiatric, or psychological condition. =302/JPT

Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. rulings.Case Title: NAOKI NITTA, INDIVIDUALLY , AS SUCCESSOR IN VS. ELDORADO NATIONAL (KANSAS), INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Renewed Motion Andmotion To Stay Civil Discovery Pending Related Criminal Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 10, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANTS REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, WALLACE RICHARDSON'S Renewed Motion And Motion To Stay Civil Discovery Pending Related Criminal Proceedings Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED as unopposed. Court requests that moving party submits either (a) an order approved as to form by opposing counsel or (b) a proposed order which contains substantially the same terms and conditions as the current existing stay order including a condition that, "Trial will not be set in this matter until a reasonable time after this stay has been lifted." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: PAUL WONG et al VS. THE OLYMPIC CLUB et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 10, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANTS THE OLYMPIC CLUB, PAUL JACQUES and GREG DEROSA's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the fourth cause of action for FEHA violations is sustained with leave to amend. Each plaintiff must comply with CRC 2.112 and segregate his/her distinct causes of action for discrimination and retaliation. Plaintiffs should also plead more facts regarding the adverse employment action. It is still unclear, as to each plaintiff, whether the Second Amended Complaint alleges that each plaintiff was terminated, constructively terminated, demoted, or whether some other adverse employment action occurred. Paragraph 43 sufficiently alleges that the plaintiffs engaged in protected activity to support the retaliation cause of action. The Court sustains the demurrer to the 5th cause of action for harassment with leave to amend. Plaintiffs shall more clearly allege that the harassment was motivated by animus pursuant to Gov't Code 12940(j). Defendants' request for judicial notice is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Punitive Damages And Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, January 10, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANTs BANK OF AMERICA N.A., CAPITOL ONE, N.A. (SUCCESSOR BY MERGER TO GREENPOINT MORTGAGE FUNDING INC.), RECONSTRUST COMPANY, N.A., GREENPOINT MORTGAGE FUNDING, INC.'S Motion To Strike Punitive Damages And Attorneys Fees. Continued to January 24, 2013 per stipulation of the parties. The matter will be heard on that day at 9:30am in Department 501. Moving party shall provide courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 501 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date no later than January 11, 2013 at 4:00 pm. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. = (302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: EGC FINANCIAL, LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY VS. MICHAEL OCHOA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order To Deem Matters Admitted
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 10, 2013, Line 7, Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order To Deem Matters Admitted Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT
